questions.text: Out of the following options, which is the most significant motivation for individuals to embark on entrepreneurship?

options.label: a
options.text: Personal fulfillment, alleviating acute pain and societal contribution

options.label: b
options.text: Maximizing economic value for the individual

options.label: c
options.text: Pursuing autonomy and being one's own boss

options.label: d
options.text: Tackling environmental challenges for global betterment and a sustainable world

questions.text: All the following statements appear to be correct to some extent. Which one of the following statements is more accurate than the rest?

options.label: a
options.text: Entrepreneurship, being highly risky, is only viable for individuals with a background in business.

options.label: b
options.text: The level of entrepreneurship in a country is a key determinant of its citizens' quality of life.

options.label: c
options.text: Engaging in entrepreneurship invariably leads to an improvement in the quality of life for entrepreneurs.

options.label: d
options.text: Extroverted individuals are predominant among successful entrepreneurs.

questions.text: What is the most significant aspect of entrepreneurs among the following?

options.label: a
options.text: Entrepreneurs generate wealth for investors.

options.label: b
options.text: Entrepreneurs primarily accumulate wealth for themselves.

options.label: c
options.text: Entrepreneurs contribute to national wealth creation in many ways.

options.label: d
options.text: Entrepreneurs play a crucial role in social advancements in a country.

questions.text: Which of the following characteristics best describes entrepreneurs?

options.label: a
options.text: Fearful of risks and uncertainties

options.label: b
options.text: Enthusiastic about taking calculated risks

options.label: c
options.text: Indifferent to risk considerations and focused on immediate gains

options.label: d
options.text: Averse to risk-taking in general

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT acknowledged as a misconception about entrepreneurship?

options.label: a
options.text: Entrepreneurs are born into business families.

options.label: b
options.text: A significant portion of entrepreneurial knowledge can be acquired through learning.

options.label: c
options.text: Entrepreneurs are typically extroverts.

options.label: d
options.text: Initiating an entrepreneurial venture demands substantial capital.

questions.text: What factor is NOT attributed to the success of Sophia Amoruso?

options.label: a
options.text: Her acute understanding of the customers

options.label: b
options.text: Her expertise as the CEO of NastyGal

options.label: c
options.text: Her social skills

options.label: d
options.text: Uncompromised and consistent ethics

questions.text: The ____ in simple words is where a company wants to reach in the long run. All members of the company must share the same _____ to reach that goal. Which of the following words is ideal to fill the above blank?

options.label: a
options.text: Vision

options.label: b
options.text: Mission

options.label: c
options.text: Strategies

options.label: d
options.text: Values

questions.text: Which among the following qualities are the LEAST requirement for employees to pursue Corporate Entrepreneurship?

options.label: a
options.text: Full empowerment to think out of the box and act like an entrepreneur without major part of the risks.

options.label: b
options.text: Should possess creativity and be innovative in conceiving new products, services, and processes and execute them effectively.

options.label: c
options.text: Action-oriented, self-driven, and creative.

options.label: d
options.text: Have managerial, technical, and interpersonal skills.

questions.text: You want to start an e-commerce company. You target the niche market of hand-made goods. You have written the following statement: "We curate and showcase a diverse array of exquisitely crafted handmade products, connecting artisans globally with discerning customers, and fostering a marketplace that celebrates craftsmanship, authenticity, and unique artistic expressions." What can this statement be termed as?

options.label: a
options.text: Vision

options.label: b
options.text: Mission

options.label: c
options.text: Strategies

options.label: d
options.text: Values

questions.text: Which of the following best defines empathy?

options.label: a
options.text: The ability to understand and share the sorrows of another and express our pain for the same.

options.label: b
options.text: The ability to feel the pain of others almost as much as we feel when we suffer the same.

options.label: c
options.text: The ability to sympathize with others for all their pains as well as celebrate their joys.

options.label: d
options.text: Never to miss a chance to say “sorry” when one comes to know about another person’s loss or pain.

questions.text: Which of the following CANNOT be concluded from the story of Dhirubhai Ambani?

options.label: a
options.text: Entrepreneurs identify opportunities in adversities.

options.label: b
options.text: Entrepreneurs start it big to be successful.

options.label: c
options.text: Entrepreneurs are knowledge seekers.

options.label: d
options.text: Entrepreneurs have a compelling vision and a comprehensive plan to execute it.

questions.text: Which of the following represents the five forces of competition as espoused by Porter?

options.label: a
options.text: Core competency, Competitive rivalry, government policy, threat of new entrant, threat of substitution

options.label: b
options.text: Competitive rivalry, government policy, buyer power, threat of substitution, threat of new entry

options.label: c
options.text: Competitive rivalry, supplier power, buyer power, threat of substitution, International competition

options.label: d
options.text: Competitive rivalry, supplier power, buyer power, threat of substitution, threat of new entrant

questions.text: Which of the following helps startups gain the LEAST amount of competitive advantage?

options.label: a
options.text: Innovative technology and protecting intellectual property rights (IPR).

options.label: b
options.text: Efficient cost management to ensure price advantage.

options.label: c
options.text: Building trusted brands with increasing loyal followers.

options.label: d
options.text: To achieve first-mover advantage through great execution.

questions.text: Identify the correct statement from the following: What is meant by the construct limited liability in the context of a limited liability form of legal entity?

options.label: a
options.text: The liability of the company is limited to its resources.

options.label: b
options.text: The liability of any owner is limited to his/her share of the business at any point in time.

options.label: c
options.text: The liability of any owner is limited to his/her proportional shareholding in the company.

options.label: d
options.text: The liability of a company is limited to the market value of its assets.

questions.text: In partnership form of business, the written agreement duly stamped and registered is known as

options.label: a
options.text: Partnership MOU

options.label: b
options.text: Memorandum of partnership

options.label: c
options.text: Partnership deed

options.label: d
options.text: Partnership agreement

questions.text: Which form of business has a limited life?

options.label: a
options.text: Limited liability partnership

options.label: b
options.text: Proprietorship

options.label: c
options.text: Private limited company

options.label: d
options.text: Public limited company

questions.text: Investors prefer which form of the legal structure of an enterprise and why? Choose the most appropriate statement from the following:

options.label: a
options.text: Proprietorship because it gives absolute liberty to the proprietor for execution and the personal assets of the proprietor can be attached in case of default.

options.label: b
options.text: Partnership because it has all attributes of proprietorships and more than one founders

options.label: c
options.text: Private limited company because the personal assets of shareholders cannot be attached to meet company obligations and it can induct new investors as shareholders.

options.label: d
options.text: Public limited since it can issue shares to the public, list its shares to stock exchanges, and the shares are easily transferable, thus offering easy exit to the investors.

questions.text: When should a startup convert its venture into a limited liability company?

options.label: a
options.text: At the idea stage

options.label: b
options.text: When the prototype is ready and customer validation is in the process

options.label: c
options.text: When the business demands such as approaching investors or incubators

options.label: d
options.text: When applying for patent or trademark or GST registration, whichever is earlier.

questions.text: What was the main reason for the failure of Iridium? Choose the best answer.

options.label: a
options.text: Over-pricing

options.label: b
options.text: Excessive capital cost

options.label: c
options.text: The technology was ahead of time.

options.label: d
options.text: Lack of product-market fit.

questions.text: Which of the following can create the least of Entry Barriers?

options.label: a
options.text: Brand loyalty

options.label: b
options.text: Business process management at the shop floor

options.label: c
options.text: Ownership of intellectual property

options.label: d
options.text: Logistic advantage

questions.text: Which of the following statements concerning market research is correct?

options.label: a
options.text: Marketing research is relevant only when the enterprise is ready with their product and is planning to go to market the product.

options.label: b
options.text: Secondary market research can be performed by designing a suitable questionnaire.

options.label: c
options.text: An enterprise does market research even before they develop the product and market research continues as long as the product exists.

options.label: d
options.text: A company should do marketing research when it reaches the saturation stage so that they can find new customers to achieve further growth.

questions.text: Which of the following statements concerning marketing research is correct?

options.label: a
options.text: Secondary data can be organized in-house through a questionnaire survey.

options.label: b
options.text: Secondary market research (gathering secondary data) is quicker than primary market research.

options.label: c
options.text: Data obtainable from secondary sources helps to understand the granular details of the customers’ needs.

options.label: d
options.text: Secondary research (gathering secondary data) involves personal interviews with the potential customers, and thus, is costlier than primary data collection.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT part of the secondary sources of data?

options.label: a
options.text: Survey

options.label: b
options.text: Census data

options.label: c
options.text: Data collected by government agencies

options.label: d
options.text: Newspaper

questions.text: Select the INCORRECT statement.

options.label: a
options.text: Markets with perfect competition have homogenous products without any pricing power.

options.label: b
options.text: Oligopolistic markets have differentiated product types with high barrier for new entrants and limited pricing power.

options.label: c
options.text: Monopolists have unique and simple type of products with great barrier for new entrants.

options.label: d
options.text: Companies in a perfect competition market have high pricing power and high entry barriers against competition.

questions.text: You are targeting a market with potential size of ₹50 crore within your geographical reach and in the exact segment that you would like to serve. The size of the aggregate market, including all sub-segments, in the whole country is ₹1000 crore. The size of the market in the market segment that you cater to in the whole country is ₹250 crore. What are TAM, SOM, & SAM?

options.label: a
options.text: ₹50 crore, ₹250 crore & ₹1000 crore

options.label: b
options.text: ₹250 crore, ₹50 crore, ₹1000 crore

options.label: c
options.text: ₹1000 crore, ₹250 crore, ₹50 crore

options.label: d
options.text: ₹1000 crore, ₹50 crore, ₹250 crore

questions.text: Which is the right chronological order of the process of customer acquisition through the sales funnel?

options.label: a
options.text: Interest, awareness, consideration, intent, evaluation, purchase

options.label: b
options.text: Consideration, awareness, interest, intent, evaluation, purchase

options.label: c
options.text: Awareness, interest, consideration, intent, evaluation, purchase

options.label: d
options.text: Intent, awareness, interest, consideration, evaluation, purchase e. Evaluation, awareness, interest, consideration, intent, purchase

questions.text: Difference between the use of public relation and advertisement in marketing. Choose the best answer.

options.label: a
options.text: Public relation is creation of awareness and is associated with the term content marketing.

options.label: b
options.text: Companies has fair amount of control on marketing through public relation.

options.label: c
options.text: Message can be sharper and more focused on the values of the product or service in case of advertisement than public relation.

options.label: d
options.text: Advertisement content is filtered through a reporter.

questions.text: Choose the right answer. Marketing funnel or sales funnel is

options.label: a
options.text: A logistic funnel through which products make their journey from the factory to the customers.

options.label: b
options.text: It is the channel through which marketing and sales personnel are promoted to higher position based on their performance and company sales.

options.label: c
options.text: It is the journey of a prospect to become a customer helping a venture to formulate strategies for customer acquisition.

options.label: d
options.text: It is the model for determining sales or revenue of an enterprise.

questions.text: The storytelling example of Huggies is about

options.label: a
options.text: Online marketing

options.label: b
options.text: Social media marketing

options.label: c
options.text: Content marketing

options.label: d
options.text: Public Relation marketing

questions.text: Which of the following purpose is not directly fulfilled by market segmentation?

options.label: a
options.text: To understand the customers, their unique requirements, and liking better

options.label: b
options.text: How to lower the cost of production and marketing

options.label: c
options.text: Understand market size, growth, and how to position your product

options.label: d
options.text: It helps to understand competition and the demand-supply gap

questions.text: Which of the following statements is wrong as regards the business model canvas?

options.label: a
options.text: The Revenue model is one of the building blocks of the Business model canvas.

options.label: b
options.text: The Value proposition is one of the building blocks of the Business Model Canvas.

options.label: c
options.text: The business model canvas tells us the ecosystem that a company is part of.

options.label: d
options.text: Business model canvas does not tell us the competitive landscape a company is in.

questions.text: Which of the following statements is NOT correct as regards the business model canvas (BMC)?

options.label: a
options.text: The value proposition is the central part of BMC and it explains how the value proposition helps the company differentiates itself from the competitors.

options.label: b
options.text: The business model canvas does not indicate the vision, mission, and value statement.

options.label: c
options.text: The market segment block explains how the company builds brands, acquires and retains customers in the long run.

options.label: d
options.text: In the Business Model Canvas (BMC), the right side focuses on the market, detailing how the company engages with customers to deliver value, while the left side outlines the internal processes and resources required to create those products or services.

questions.text: Which of the following does not serve by market segmentation?

options.label: a
options.text: To understand the customers better

options.label: b
options.text: To create product differentiation

options.label: c
options.text: To improve the assessment of post-sales customer experiences.

options.label: d
options.text: It helps to understand competition and the demand-supply gap

questions.text: Choose the correct answer. Innovation and invention are not the same. Innovation is:

options.label: a
options.text: Developing new technologies with novelties, not obvious to people with adequate technical knowledge in the relevant domain.

options.label: b
options.text: Developing new technology and the infrastructure necessary to make the solution available and making the benefits available to those who need them.

options.label: c
options.text: Developing new cutting-edge technology that is yet to emerge in the market and that is far ahead of time.

options.label: d
options.text: Developing solutions that do a lot of good to society.

questions.text: In the early 1900s, General Electric (GE) of Thomas Edison disciplined the chaotic process of scientific discovery into a systematic process of innovation. Results: over the next 70 years, won more patents than any other company in the US. Much of GE’s competitive prowess during the century following can be traced to that extraordinary accomplishment. Which of the following most accurately describes the above revolutionary act of GE?

options.label: a
options.text: This is a technological innovation that gave General Electric many patents.

options.label: b
options.text: This is an invention that gave the end-beneficiary enormous advantages and thus GE enjoyed competitive advantages for many years to come.

options.label: c
options.text: This is an invention of business process management.

options.label: d
options.text: This is an example of research process innovation.

questions.text: In 1903, DuPont systematized capital-budgeting techniques. A few years later, the company also developed a standardized way of estimating the return on investment. They used it in comparing the performance of its numerous product departments. These innovations and more helped DuPont become one of America’s industrial giants.

options.label: a
options.text: This is a financial management innovation and falls under management innovation.

options.label: b
options.text: This is not an innovation but a core part of business process management that gave DuPont much competitive advantage.

options.label: c
options.text: This is an innovation in funding R&D activities giving DuPont competitive advantages for many years to come.

options.label: d
options.text: This is an accounting invention that helped DuPont cut cost and time of building new products.

questions.text: Business model innovation is least possible through which of the following?

options.label: a
options.text: Changing the value proposition based on customers’ changing aspirations.

options.label: b
options.text: Changing business system architecture to improve process efficiencies.

options.label: c
options.text: Through management innovation of various kind such as improvement in logistics, aftersales service, business process, etc.

options.label: d
options.text: Engaging in R&D for improvement in environment management in general and effective delivery of corporate social responsibilities.

questions.text: Uber could define the pain points and it could apply the latest technologies to solve them. In urban places of high mobility, it is very difficult to get a taxi: one that would come and pick you up, one with some identity for reliability and safety, to know when a taxi will arrive, ease of paying for the tip. They innovated the solution based on the pains. The above text is related to which block in the business model canvas?

options.label: a
options.text: Customer segment

options.label: b
options.text: Key activities

options.label: c
options.text: Key partners

options.label: d
options.text: Value proposition e. Revenue model

questions.text: How do you promote customer experience management?

options.label: a
options.text: Comparing your own performance and the performance of competitors and taking appropriate actions to ensure your performance is better than that of the competitors.

options.label: b
options.text: By gathering data on customers’ purchase behavior, their browsing patterns, and frequency of visit, and purchase execution frequency and responding accordingly.

options.label: c
options.text: You sell products with the best quality, at the lowest price, and best aesthetic look so that customers have nothing to complain and they do not buy products of competitors.

options.label: d
options.text: Offer free gifts to your customers so that they are pleasantly surprised and come back to buy repeatedly.

questions.text: Which of the following statements is most appropriate?

options.label: a
options.text: A business model is mostly a written document or slide deck delineating the process of execution, marketing and growth plan, and the projected financial performance.

options.label: b
options.text: A business model describes the rationale of how an organization creates, delivers, and captures value, in economic, social, cultural, or other contexts.

options.label: c
options.text: A business model describes the rationale of how an organization develops products or services using modern cutting-edge and futuristic technologies.

options.label: d
options.text: A business model describes the rationale of how an organization builds a team, identifies opportunities, and goes-to-market.

questions.text: In his book Crossing the Chasm, Geoffrey Moore explained that a majority of startups fail even after raising a good amount of funds and acquiring a substantial number of customers terming the event as CHASM. When does the chasm occur? Choose the best answer.

options.label: a
options.text: As a company grows, attains maturity, the growth diminishes and the product adoption curve moves downward. If such a company does not relaunch an improved version of the product, the downward movement continues and the product fails to sustain.

options.label: b
options.text: As a company go-to-market, it acquires customers of the innovator kind and the early adopter kind, it finds itself stressed in managing logistics, supplies, customer service, marketing resources, and other business process management. If the company is not well prepared to handle such challenges, it fails to move ahead.

options.label: c
options.text: As a company grows and starts acquiring customers from the early majority to the late majority, it fails to recruit enough employees to cater to or serve a growing number of customers. So, the chasm sets in.

options.label: d
options.text: The chasm is defined as the critical phase of product development when more potential customers disagree to validate the product than those who agree and the company fails to raise enough funds to continue its efforts in taking the process forward.

questions.text: The world and the aspiration of people are ever-changing. The changes offer a unique opportunity for new companies to preemptively build products or services to fulfill the changing needs. Companies that are ahead of the trend frequently become winners. Such pioneering companies integrate trending things into their business model setting them apart and keeping them vibrant. Which of the following sentences is correct as regard business trends? Choose the one that appears the most appropriate.

options.label: a
options.text: You must follow the trend to identify a winning business model.

options.label: b
options.text: Startups who operate at the forefront of the trend enjoy first-mover advantages and always become successful.

options.label: c
options.text: One must start a business based on the forecast of the technology trend in the respective field to achieve definite success.

options.label: d
options.text: First-movers enjoy some advantages by remaining ahead of a trend, but to be successful and remain a winner first-mover advantages alone are not good enough.

questions.text: Several factors are responsible for the chasm in the life cycle of a company that is difficult for many to cross. Which of the following factors is the least responsible to cause the chasm?

options.label: a
options.text: During the growth phase many ventures fail to arrange the required infrastructure for increasing activities to meet the growing demand resulting in dissatisfied customers, which leads to decreasing number of customers and eventual failure.

options.label: b
options.text: The products are at an early stage that is liked by the early adopters but the early majority of customers are less forgiving and they look for more perfection. Many ventures fail to fulfill their requirements and thus fail to attract more customers.

options.label: c
options.text: In some companies, the disharmony among the co-founders leads to great friction giving rise to a chasm in the minds of the founders that becomes difficult to cross leading to the death of a startup.

options.label: d
options.text: Product not gaining acceptance by the early majority of the customers and lack of organizational preparedness to manage growth.

questions.text: Which of the following statements is the most appropriate regarding business process management?

options.label: a
options.text: Business process management involves discovering, modeling, analyzing, measuring, improving, optimizing, and automating business processes. To achieve growth beyond early adopters an enterprise needs a professional CEO to manage the business processes.

options.label: b
options.text: It is most critically important at the idea selection phase for a clear understanding of business processes that the execution of the business model will warrant.

options.label: c
options.text: It is the most critically important aspect of business for production process management.

options.label: d
options.text: In the digital age, business process management is the most critically important for business information management.

questions.text: Which among the following is NOT a correct statement?

options.label: a
options.text: If you start a business in the domain of your circle of competence you will have better possibility of success.

options.label: b
options.text: One may not be a genius, but may achieve great success by focusing and remaining within his/her circle of competence.

options.label: c
options.text: The circle of competency is a domain where your knowledge is the best compared to your proficiency in other domains.

options.label: d
options.text: The circle of competence is the core area of the knowledge domain of your key employees using which you can make a great success.

questions.text: What is meant by a validated learning process?

options.label: a
options.text: It involves steps in product development wherein feedback from potential customers is obtained at different stages of the development process.

options.label: b
options.text: The validated learning process is learning through the process that has been validated by experts and the risk of failure is minimized.

options.label: c
options.text: The validated learning process is a process of product development, wherein an enterprise builds a product by learning from the steps validated and recommended by experts, thereby reducing the chances of rejection of the product by customers.

options.label: d
options.text: It is a linear process of product development such as the waterfall model wherein a prototype of the product is validated by potential customers and the prototype is refined based on the learning of such validation process.

questions.text: Which of the following best defines the Agile Process?

options.label: a
options.text: The agile process dictates reducing waste at all steps and stages to make the business process lean and more competitive. The process mandates that if any part of the product can be eliminated without negatively affecting customers’ perception of value, then it should be eliminated.

options.label: b
options.text: It is a non-linear process of product development that follows the strategy of reduction of process time and raw-materials requirement, and adding features to attract more customers.

options.label: c
options.text: Agile process is a linear process in which a large project is broken into components and development progresses in regular collaboration with the targeted users.

options.label: d
options.text: The agile process is a component of the lean startup strategy that involves breaking the product development process into small components, early onboarding the customer in the product development process, and obtaining feedback to refine the execution.

questions.text: A startup begins with two hypotheses: a value hypothesis and a growth hypothesis. Which of the following is NOT in conformity with the value hypothesis? Choose the most appropriate option.

options.label: a
options.text: The value hypothesis is that the company will attain a valuation that is several times the investment made by all investors including that by the founders.

options.label: b
options.text: The proposed product will be superior compared to those of the competitors in terms of values perceived by the customers.

options.label: c
options.text: The value hypothesis states that the quality and features of the product or service offered by the company will be perceived by the customers as superior to those of the competitors.

options.label: d
options.text: The value hypothesis is a presumption that customers will receive superior value for their money when they buy the company’s product or service.

questions.text: A startup begins with a leap of faith. What is a leap of faith in this regard?

options.label: a
options.text: It is an assumption, without any data to support such assumption, that the product will sell in a market where there is no precedence of such a product.

options.label: b
options.text: A leap of faith is belief in God and remaining positive that if we are honest God will make us successful.

options.label: c
options.text: A leap of faith is a risky but calculated hypothesis about a favorable action by potential customers while launching a new product.

options.label: d
options.text: A leap of faith is taking a leap in the faith of becoming successful based on limited data and credentials.

questions.text: Select all the correct options from the following in respect of the lean startup concept.

options.label: a
options.text: A, B & C

options.label: b
options.text: B & C

options.label: c
options.text: B & D

options.label: d
options.text: A, B, & D

questions.text: Identify the correct statement from the following:

options.label: a
options.text: Design thinking formalizes the process of innovative product development by introducing a systematic step-by-step methodology to define a problem and follow a validated learning process.

options.label: b
options.text: Leap of faith is a kind of hypothesis based on limited data that a startup relies on to proceed with product development and create a new venture.

options.label: c
options.text: Design thinking presupposes that some people are inherently creative and become successful in creative product development. The team should have at least one such person.

options.label: d
options.text: Design thinking is a convergence-divergence process that encourages a large idea around a problem with the understanding no idea is unimportant.

questions.text: Which of the following statements is correct?

options.label: a
options.text: Design thinking is a convergence-divergence process

options.label: b
options.text: One can generate creative ideas by applying the design thinking process and thus can develop innovative products and processes.

options.label: c
options.text: Design thinking does not help in creative idea generation but stimulates product development teams generating many ideas some of which may lead to inventions.

options.label: d
options.text: Design thinking is predominantly a product development process and is not very suitable for service innovation.

questions.text: Which of the following is not strictly one of the profiles of design thinkers?

options.label: a
options.text: Empathy

options.label: b
options.text: Lateral or out of the box thinker

options.label: c
options.text: Integrative thinker

options.label: d
options.text: Experimentalism

questions.text: Which of the following is the most impactful statement about open innovation?

options.label: a
options.text: By sharing the capabilities, resources, and talents between collaborators, open innovation process helps to significantly reduce product development time and cost.

options.label: b
options.text: Open innovation failed because of the possibility of disputes over the intellectual properties of the participating enterprises.

options.label: c
options.text: It is better to choose partners or collaborators from the same country as cultural diversities may induce mistrust and may not lead to real new products as desired.

options.label: d
options.text: Open innovation process is a sure-shot method to beat the competition since it ensures quick and successful development of new products.

questions.text: Which of the following is true?

options.label: a
options.text: TRIZ involves 40 contradictions and 39 inventive principles.

options.label: b
options.text: Primary steps in TRIZ involve brainstorming, synectics, lateral thinking, and neuro-linguistic programming.

options.label: c
options.text: One of the major limitations of TRIZ is that it cannot overcome psychological inertia, which is due to habits, education, paradigms, internal processes, past successes, past failures.

options.label: d
options.text: The primary premise of TRIZ is that one can learn how to innovate.

questions.text: Which of the following is not true?

options.label: a
options.text: Synectics is a process of communication in the subconscious state of the creative side of our brain with the conscious brain and that the cognitive side has no role in the process.

options.label: b
options.text: While developing a new product, building a prototype enables a practical engagement with the users and you get deeper empathy and realistic feedback.

options.label: c
options.text: The meanings that a product or service might have for its users include the memories it invokes, the extent and quality of interaction, the user experience, and enjoyment.

options.label: d
options.text: Technological innovations are important for business success. But the capability to innovate meaning can add unique value that is difficult to emulate.

questions.text: All the following statements about entrepreneurs are true to varied degrees. Select the one that defines entrepreneurs the least.

options.label: a
options.text: Entrepreneurs identify opportunities around a major pain or adversities and create solutions to alleviate the pain or adversities.

options.label: b
options.text: Entrepreneurs are capable of identifying opportunities in adversities, estimating the risk, taking calculated steps to minimize risks, and executing smartly.

options.label: c
options.text: Entrepreneurs prefer to manage people in an office setting rather than working for a supervisor in a large corporation.

options.label: d
options.text: Entrepreneurs create successful businesses by offering solutions that are superior to those offered by competitors and giving higher value to customers.

questions.text: Which of the following statements is the most appropriate (your answer should be based on the lecture materials only)?

options.label: a
options.text: Maximum percentage of companies fail because of lack of product-market fit.

options.label: b
options.text: Lack of necessary funds for scaling is the cause of failure of the largest percentage of startups.

options.label: c
options.text: Maximum percentage of startups fail because they are not thrifty.

options.label: d
options.text: Disharmony among founders is not a significant reason for the failure of startups.

questions.text: Which of the following arguments on the difference between Small Businesses and Entrepreneurial ventures is not correct?

options.label: a
options.text: Entrepreneurial ventures target high growth, whereas small businesses usually achieve low growth.

options.label: b
options.text: Entrepreneurial ventures are innovation-driven, whereas small businesses do not involve innovation.

options.label: c
options.text: Small businesses have a long-term business perspective, whereas entrepreneurial ventures target quick growth and success.

options.label: d
options.text: Small businesses can translate themselves into entrepreneurial ventures by radically changing their business models using innovative steps.

questions.text: Identify the correct definition of Synectic from the following.

options.label: a
options.text: Understanding how the cognitive conscious part of the brain interacts with the conscious creative part of the brain.

options.label: b
options.text: The art of joining pieces of information to create new knowledge through collaboration between the conscious and the subconscious part of the brain.

options.label: c
options.text: The to-and-fro communication and dynamic validation between the challenges around a creative solution and the myriad of ideas generated by our brains.

options.label: d
options.text: Collaboration between the cognitive and the creative part of the brain at the subconscious zone of the brain in order to find a solution to any problem that the conscious brain is striving to find.

questions.text: Use the following data to answer the question following them: (All figures are in Rupees except when specifically mentioned otherwise) Sales: 3,000 Purchase of equipment: 50 Closing Stock: 500 Opening Stock: 1,000 Payment of electricity bill: 30 Purchase of goods: 1500 What is the gross profit?

The following data are related to a company for the financial year 2021-2022. (All figures are in Rupees except when specifically mentioned otherwise) Amortization: 100 Interest payment: 200 Income tax: 100 Depreciation: 50 Other operating expenses: 500 Dividend: 200 Gross profit: 1,200 What is the operating profit for the year 2021-22? (be mindful of excess data)

The following data are related to a company for the financial year 2021-2022. (All figures are in Rupees except when specifically mentioned otherwise) Gross profit: 500 Interest payment: 50 Amortization: 50 Other operating expenses: 200 Dividend: 100 The book value of the fixed asset of the company as of 31-3-2021 is Rs. 1,300. The depreciation rate is 10% using the declining value method. The company purchases machinery worth Rs. 700 during the year 2021-22 and does not sell any of its fixed assets during the year. What is the book value of the fixed assets as of 31.03.2022? Be mindful of excess data.
Which of the following is not part of the operating expense?

options.label: a
options.text: Interest

options.label: b
options.text: Salary

options.label: c
options.text: Amortization

options.label: d
options.text: Depreciation

questions.text: Identify the correct statement.

options.label: a
options.text: Both the balance sheet and profit & loss account pertain to a financial year.

options.label: b
options.text: The cash flow statement pertains to a period of time.

options.label: c
options.text: The balance sheet of a company pertains to a period of time such as a financial year.

options.label: d
options.text: The profit & loss account pertains to a particular date. e. The cash flow statement pertains to a particular date.

questions.text: A company sells pens. Following information pertains to its operation in a particular year (All data are in Rupees except when otherwise mentioned): Per unit selling price of pen: ₹10 Number of pens sold during the year: 100 Closing Stock: ₹100 Depreciation: ₹100 Opening Stock: ₹50 Amortization: ₹100 Purchase of goods: ₹800 What is the gross profit margin during this period?

Following data are related to a company for the financial year 2016-2017. (All figures are in Rupees except when specifically mentioned otherwise) Sales: 1,000 Gross profit: 800 Interest payment: 50 Depreciation: 50 Salary: 250 Rent: 50 Other operating expenses: 200 Dividend: 100 What is the operating profit margin for the company during 2016-17?

Following data are related to a company for the financial year 2021-2022. (all figures are in Rupees except when specifically mentioned otherwise) Gross profit: 800 Interest payment: 50 Depreciation: 50 Other operating expenses: 400 Dividend: 100 Assume that the company doesn’t pay any income tax, what is the retained profit?

Following data are related to a company for the financial year 2021-2022. (all figures are in Rupees except when specifically mentioned otherwise) Gross profit: 600 Amortization: 10 Interest payment: 50 Depreciation: 40 Other operating expenses: 200 Dividend: 100 If the income tax is 30% what is the EBITDA?

The following data are related to a company for the financial year 2021-2022. (All figures are in Rupees except when specifically mentioned otherwise) Gross profit: 950 Interest payment: 50 Depreciation: 50 Other operating expenses: 250 Dividend: 100 Reserves and surplus as of 31-3-2021: 900 The company pays income tax @30% What is the reserves and surplus of the company as of 31-3-2022?

The following data are related to a particular company for the year 2021-2022: Fixed cost per year: 2000 Variable cost for the year: 1500 Number of units produced and sold during the year: 100 Unit selling price: 20 The break-even point is:
Which of the following is definitely not an assumption in break-even point analysis?

options.label: a
options.text: The cost can be divided into fixed and variable.

options.label: b
options.text: Change in volume of sales does not change in fixed cost.

options.label: c
options.text: Stock consumption or Cost of Goods Sold is equal to opening stock plus purchase during the year minus closing stock.

options.label: d
options.text: Change in the volume of sales does not affect the price of the product.

questions.text: The following data are related to a company for the financial year 2021-2022. Gross profit: 1000 Interest payment: 50 Operating expenses excluding depreciation: 350 Dividend: 100 The book value of the fixed asset of the company as of 31-3-2021 was 300. The depreciation rate is 10%. The company purchases machinery worth 700 during the year 2021-22 and sells existing fixed assets of book value of 500 during the same year. What is the operating profit of the company during 2021-22?

The following data pertain to a particular year of operation of a company. Sales for the year: 10000 The variable cost for the year: 6000 Fixed cost for the year: 1000 Number of units produced and sold during the year: 400 What is the margin of safety?
Estimate the contribution margin in Rupees per unit using the following data. 1. Sales: ₹5000 2. Number of units produced and sold: 100 3. Total variable cost: ₹2000 4. Annual fixed cost: ₹1000
For a company, the fixed cost F = 40,000 for a year, the variable cost V = 20 per unit, selling price S = 30 per unit. How many units the company should produce and sell to make a profit of ₹10,000?
By applying a lean start-up process a company has reduced its variable cost by 10% without changing the fixed cost. What will happen to the operating leverage?

options.label: a
options.text: It will go up

options.label: b
options.text: It will go down

options.label: c
options.text: No change since operating leverage is related to fixed cost.

options.label: d
options.text: The question is inappropriate since both fixed cost and variable cost are unrelated to operating leverage.

questions.text: Which one of the following is not a direct expense?

options.label: a
options.text: Raw material

options.label: b
options.text: Selling and marketing

options.label: c
options.text: Packaging

options.label: d
options.text: Wages

questions.text: All units are in Rupees. The unit selling price of a product: 300 Total all-inclusive variable cost per unit: 200 Total all-inclusive fixed cost per year: 2,00,000 How much profit the company makes if it produces and sells 3,000 units of products?
Which of the following is correct?

options.label: a
options.text: The profit before tax is the money that belongs to the owners

options.label: b
options.text: A positive margin of safety does not necessarily mean that the company is operating in profit.

options.label: c
options.text: A company’s capacity to absorb raw-material price fluctuations can be gauged from the difference in the slopes of the sales line and total cost line.

options.label: d
options.text: Financial leverage is the degree to which a firm can increase operating income by increasing sales.

questions.text: Arrange the following in the order of importance in the context of the success of a startup as suggested by Bill Gross (most important first).

options.label: a
options.text: Team, Timing, Business model, Team

options.label: b
options.text: Team, Timing, idea, execution

options.label: c
options.text: Timing, Team, Idea, execution

options.label: d
options.text: Timing, Team, execution, Idea

questions.text: What is meant by complementary skills? Choose the best answer.

options.label: a
options.text: A team of cofounders with different skills so that all the skills that are necessary for executing the business are available.

options.label: b
options.text: A team with all the skillsets demanded by the concurrent industries so that a startup can diversify into any domain of its choice without concern of technology.

options.label: c
options.text: The skillset complementary to that demanded by the changing technological landscape in the market.

options.label: d
options.text: All the team members having all the skills similar to those demanded by the startup.

questions.text: Which of the following is the correct interpretation of the construct insurgent mission in the context of entrepreneurship mentality?

options.label: a
options.text: You are in a blue ocean market and are on a mission to capture enough customers before it turns into a red ocean.

options.label: b
options.text: You are on a mission to seize customers from competitors and acquire them by hook or crook and no rule applies to you.

options.label: c
options.text: You are on a mission to wage a war with the customers and want to win them over at any cost.

options.label: d
options.text: You are at war against competitors on behalf of the underserved customers and would not stop until you win.

questions.text: Which of the following is arranged in correct chronology in the context of applying Design Thinking?

options.label: a
options.text: Empathize, Ideate, Define, Prototype, Test

options.label: b
options.text: Empathize, Prototype, Define, Ideate, Test

options.label: c
options.text: Define, Empathize, Ideate, Prototype, Test

options.label: d
options.text: Empathize, Define, Ideate, Prototype, Test

questions.text: Which of the following is not true?

options.label: a
options.text: Invention is the creation of new knowledge and innovation is the creation of new knowledge as also the commercial application of the new knowledge for the benefit of the relevant people.

options.label: b
options.text: Innovation is creating new knowledge, whereas invention is putting the invention into public use.

options.label: c
options.text: Innovation is how the entrepreneur either creates new wealth-producing resources or endows existing resources with enhanced potential for creating wealth.

options.label: d
options.text: A leap of faith is an assumption of a future solution without any supporting data whereas, a hypothesis is an assumption based on limited data or evidence.

questions.text: Which of the following is not an essential component of the elevator pitch or speech?

options.label: a
options.text: An elevator pitch should be no longer than 60 seconds, ideally 20 to 30 seconds long.

options.label: b
options.text: In an elevator pitch, you should explain why you think that you have a winning proposition, what is your USP and what are your competitive advantages.

options.label: c
options.text: Speak as fast as you can since the time is short and you want to convey as much information as possible.

options.label: d
options.text: Remain open to an interjection by your audience at any time.

questions.text: Which of the following is the correct interpretation of the construct owners’ mindset in the context of entrepreneurship?

options.label: a
options.text: A, C, D

options.label: b
options.text: A, B, D

options.label: c
options.text: A, B, C

options.label: d
options.text: All the options are part of the owners’ mindset.

questions.text: Guy Kawasaki recommended

options.label: a
options.text: Twenty-minute presentation, ten slides, and minimum thirty font size

options.label: b
options.text: Twenty-minute presentation, thirty slides, and minimum ten font size

options.label: c
options.text: Thirty-minute presentation, twenty slides, and minimum ten font size

options.label: d
options.text: Ten-minute presentation, twenty slides, and minimum thirty font size

questions.text: Which of the following is the correct interpretation of the construct frontline obsession in the context of entrepreneurship?

options.label: a
options.text: B, C, & D

options.label: b
options.text: A, & D

options.label: c
options.text: A, B, & C

options.label: d

questions.text: For a particular company: Sales: ₹50,000 Total annual variable cost: ₹40,000 Number of units produced and sold: 5,000 Total annual fixed cost: ₹2,00,000 How many units of the product the company must produce and sell to make a profit of ₹2,00,000?
Which of the following analogy is NOT correct about raising money through debt, preference shares, and equity?

options.label: a
options.text: Preference shareholder’s voting right is limited to their percentage holding in total preference shares of the company.

options.label: b
options.text: Debt is repayable whereas there is no such provision in the case of equity.

options.label: c
options.text: Money in the form of debt requires to be secured by providing collaterals in most cases, whereas, no such security is to be provided in the case of equity.

options.label: d
options.text: Interest is payable in the case of debt, whereas there is no such requirement in the case of equity.

questions.text: A business angel invests 100 million in a startup for a pre-money valuation of 400 million. The angel has 2X liquidity and participatory preference. The company later has a liquidity event (is sold) for 800 million. How much money the founders will receive?

options.label: a
options.text: 600 million

options.label: b
options.text: 480 million

options.label: c
options.text: 640 million

options.label: d
options.text: 360 million

questions.text: Choose the correct option. What is the difference between primary and collateral securities?

options.label: a
options.text: Primary security is a charge on the assets created out of the loan and collateral security relates to a charge on assets other than those created out of the loan.

options.label: b
options.text: Collateral security is a charge on the assets created out of the loan and primary security relates to a charge on assets other than those created out of the loan.

options.label: c
options.text: Primary security is a charge on liquid assets whereas collateral securities is a charge on illiquid assets.

options.label: d
options.text: Primary securities are the assets of the co-founders whereas collateral securities are the assets of the company.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT correct about term sheet?

options.label: a
options.text: It is a non-binding document containing the key terms of the proposed investment.

options.label: b
options.text: It lays out the basic conditions under which an investment will be made.

options.label: c
options.text: It is the final share subscription agreement executed after legal due diligence.

options.label: d
options.text: It helps avoid misunderstanding between founders and investors before definitive agreements.

questions.text: Which of the following is correct regarding dilution?

options.label: a
options.text: Dilution always reduces the monetary value of founders’ holding.

options.label: b
options.text: Dilution means the founders’ ownership percentage reduces when new shares are issued.

options.label: c
options.text: Dilution applies only when debt is converted into equity.

options.label: d
options.text: Dilution is illegal unless approved by investors only.

questions.text: If a company raises money through equity, which of the following is most appropriate?

options.label: a
options.text: The company must pay fixed interest irrespective of profit.

options.label: b
options.text: The investor necessarily gets collateral security over fixed assets.

options.label: c
options.text: The investor gets ownership in the company in return for the investment.

options.label: d
options.text: The founders must repay the principal within a fixed tenure.

questions.text: If investors invest ₹500 million at a pre-money valuation of ₹500 million through participating preference shares with no liquidation preference and the company later has a liquidity event of ₹5000 million, how much would the founders receive?

options.label: a
options.text: ₹500 million

options.label: b
options.text: ₹1500 million

options.label: c
options.text: ₹2500 million

options.label: d
options.text: ₹4500 million

questions.text: Choose the right statement.

options.label: a
options.text: Long-term loan from banks is for the working capital requirement.

options.label: b
options.text: The short-term loan from banks is for the procurement of fixed assets.

options.label: c
options.text: Only long-term loan from banks is related to equity dilution.

options.label: d
options.text: Long-term loan from banks is repayable in installments along with interest.

questions.text: A company raises 150 million rupees at a pre-money valuation of 450 million rupees in the form of a simple participating preference share with 3x liquidation preference. A few years later the company realizes a liquidity event and is acquired for 1000 million rupees. How much money the founders receive?

options.label: a
options.text: 514.5 million

options.label: b
options.text: 655.0 million

options.label: c
options.text: 412.5 million

options.label: d
options.text: 523.6 million

questions.text: A business angel invests 300 million in a venture at a pre-money valuation of 700 million in participating preference shares (no liquidation preference). The venture also raises funds of 100 million from a bank as a long-term loan and the outstanding balance in the loan account is 100 million. At this point, the company is liquidated for 350 million. How much money the founders will receive if the investor continues to hold the preference shares?

options.text: 175 million

options.label: b
options.text: 0 million

options.label: c
options.text: Negative (-) 50 million (Founders have to pay 50 million to the angel)

options.label: d

options.label: a
options.text: A & C

options.label: b
options.text: A & D

options.label: c
options.text: B & D

options.label: d
options.text: C & D

questions.text: Which of the following statements is incorrect?

options.label: a
options.text: A project may look attractive based on the discounted net present value method but may not be so based on break-even analysis.

options.label: b
options.text: The hurdle rate is the minimum rate of return below which a company will not accept a project.

options.label: c
options.text: The break-even point estimation takes into account the time value of money.

options.label: d
options.text: The payback period method does not take into account the time value of money.

questions.text: An article appeared in the Vogue journal with the title “Netflix of Eye Wear” and that led to a huge number of customer registration for purchasing the product. options.label: a
options.text: Public relations

options.label: b
options.text: Content marketing

options.label: c
options.text: Social media

options.label: d
options.text: Advertisement e. Influencer marketing

questions.text: Which of the following is not part of a great launch?

options.label: a
options.text: Empathizing to define the pain-point

options.label: b
options.text: Timing and cross-functional readiness

options.label: c
options.text: Influencers/Customers

options.label: d
options.text: Pre-brief e. Messaging

questions.text: Which of the following is not part of the go-to-market strategies?

options.label: a
options.text: Market segmentation and strategic product roadmap, marketing strategies.

options.label: b
options.text: Product-market-fit, competitive landscape, and pricing model.

options.label: c
options.text: Distribution/ Channel/ Partner model.

options.label: d
options.text: Engagement with the early majority of customers for achieving growth.

questions.text: Which of the following is not true?

options.label: a
options.text: Customer acquisition cost must be less than the lifetime value.

options.label: b
options.text: If our business does not offer the choice for a customer to come back repeatedly, we should not spend as much money to acquire customers as we earn from that customer in the first transaction.

options.label: c
options.text: The number of registered users is definitely regarded as an important metric that can be highlighted during a presentation before investors since investors would view that as a guaranteed success factor.

options.label: d
options.text: Network-based companies such as Amazon & Flipkart can afford to spend a lot of money to acquire customers at a high burn rate since they offer many choices to customers to come back repeatedly.

questions.text: Which of the following is not part of the capital budgeting decision process?

options.label: a
options.text: Comparing two or more business models

options.label: b
options.text: Estimating the acceptability of a project based on the expected return

options.label: c
options.text: Selection between multiple machines based on the cash flow attributed to the respective machines.

options.label: d
options.text: Estimating the break-even point

questions.text: Which of the following statements is NOT correct about capital budgeting?

options.label: a
options.text: It helps us to assess the performance of managers in the efficient deployment of capital.

options.label: b
options.text: It helps entrepreneurs to understand the comparative merits of business opportunities.

options.label: c
options.text: Though information for applying capital budgeting techniques is futuristic, it provides useful information for decision-making.

options.label: d
options.text: Helps in selection between multiple machines and buy or lease decisions.

questions.text: A business contract requires ₹700 initial investment (year zero). It is likely to generate an annual net cash flow of ₹200 for five years beginning from year one and return no salvage value at the end of the business horizon. If required, use the rate of return @ 10%. Estimate the net present value in rounded full Rupee? The present value multiplication factor for 10% discount rate for 5 years is 3.79.
What is hurdle rate?

options.label: a
options.text: It is the internal rate of return of a project.

options.label: b
options.text: It is the rate at which the payback period is estimated by discounting the future cash flow.

options.label: c
options.text: It is the minimum rate of return on equity from a project for it to be favorably considered.

options.label: d
options.text: It is the minimum rate of return that a project must generate for it to be accepted by a company.

questions.text: Which of the following is not correct?

options.label: a
options.text: Early recruits can fill key knowledge gaps. Human resource experts can help identify and recruit such persons, and thus, having a dedicated HR department will do wonders for getting your startup off the ground.

options.label: b
options.text: HR people can identify great talents, attract them, integrate them with the company, and design a combination of a financial and intellectual package that will help in retaining them.

options.label: c
options.text: HR personnel have all-round business views and can prepare a wonderful business plan for presentation before investors and other stakeholders.

options.label: d
options.text: Startups frequently get embroiled in legal issues that use up a good part of their precious time. An HR team can help them prevent committing any breach of the laws of the land.

questions.text: Which one of the following statements is not correct regarding the startup valuation?

options.label: a
options.text: The early-stage startup valuation is done based on the discounted cash flow model.

options.label: b
options.text: The future cash flows for startups lack visibility.

options.label: c
options.text: Startups mostly are not in profit during the early years.

options.label: d

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT true about equity capital?

options.label: a
options.text: The paid-up capital is the portion of the authorized capital that shareholders have subscribed to by paying for their share of the equity shares in full.

options.label: b
options.text: When a company requires to raise equity capital beyond the existing authorized capital, it has to take the permission of the Registrar of Companies after such raising.

options.label: c
options.text: Owners’ equity is the sum total of Paid-up Equity Capital and Reserves & Surplus.

options.label: d
options.text: Equity capital consists of the number of equity shares with equal face values of a certain denomination.

questions.text: A company has an authorized capital of Rupees 10,000. A, B, and C are the three cofounder shareholders. A holds 20 shares of Rupees 100 each, B holds 10 shares of Rupees 100 each, and C holds 20 shares of Rupees 100 each. There is no other shareholder in the company. What are their percentage holdings in the company?

options.label: a
options.text: 20%, 10% & 20% respectively

options.label: b
options.text: 40%, 10% & 40% respectively

options.label: c
options.text: 26.67%, 6.67%, and 26.67% respectively

options.label: d
options.text: 40%, 20% & 40% respectively

questions.text: The company, Your Company, has an authorized capital of Rupees 1000. A, B, and C are the three cofounder shareholders. A holds 20 shares of Rupees 10 each, B holds 10 shares of Rupees 10 each, and C holds 20 shares of Rupees 10 each. There is no other shareholder in the company. Your Company has negotiated an investment round with an angel. The angel has valued Your Company at Rupees 10,000 before investment. They agreed to invest Rupees 10,000 for 50% of the company (equity). How Your Company will issue shares to the angel to avail of this investment? Choose the most appropriate option.

options.label: a
options.text: All cofounders give away 50% of their shares to the angel. Therefore, the cofounder together holds 50%, and the angel holds 50% after the allotment of shares.

options.label: b
options.text: Your company creates 50 new shares of Rupees 10 and allot to the angel.

options.label: c
options.text: Your company creates 500 new shares of Rupees 10 and allot to the angel.

options.label: d
options.text: Your company creates 100 new shares of Rupees 100 and allot to the angel.

questions.text: An angel agrees to invest Rupees 40 million in a startup at a pre-money valuation of Rupees 120 million. All the cofounders together hold 750 shares of Rupees 10 each. This is the first-time investment for the company and there is no other existing shareholder at present. How many shares are to be allotted to the angel for this round of investment?
